title: Acronia teaguei
intro: Acronia teaguei, also known as the Teague's acronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Acronia teaguei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Acronia teaguei is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions due to its reported efficacy.
taxonomy: Acronia teaguei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the regions of Borneo and the Philippines. Acronia teaguei belongs to the genus Acronia, which is a group of plants within the Rubiaceae family.
